_comment: Gold set for the FULL Obsidian smoke (tests/run_smoke.sh <flavour> full).
Every query below has a near-miss neighbour in the vault — that's the point:
'compton scattering' must beat Inverse-Compton and Thomson, 'oort cloud' must
beat Oort Constants. A lite 2-note fixture cannot test any of this.
max_rank: the expected page must land at least this high; lower but inside
top_k is a WARN (ranking slip), outside top_k is a FAIL (retrieval broken).
Baseline at the pinned revision (bge-m3, Qwen3.5-9B chat): 13/13 pass, with
2 standing WARNs recorded in _baseline below. A THIRD warn is a regression.

lyman alpha forest._baseline: KNOWN SLIP: rank 4, outranked by Alpha Process / Alpha Decay / Triple-Alpha Process — 'alpha' pulls the nucleosynthesis cluster. Kept at max_rank 1 deliberately: it is the correct expectation, and the standing WARN is the record of a real weakness. A slip past rank 5 would FAIL.

gravitational lensing._baseline: KNOWN SLIP: rank 3, outranked by Detection Methods / Cosmic Microwave Background (both discuss lensing).

maximum mass of a white dwarf._near_miss: Schoenberg Chandrasekhar Limit, Degeneracy Pressure, Tolman-Oppenheimer-Volkoff Limit
maximum mass of a white dwarf._note: paraphrase, not a title match — this is the query the dense index has to earn

how are exoplanets detected._note: paraphrase; the page title shares no words with the query
